serverStatus.errorSubtitle: The backend service may not be running or is unreachable
serverStatus.retryFailed: Connection failed. Please check the backend service.

hints.devPort: Confirm backend is listening on port 4000
hints.electronRestart: Try restarting the app; check main process logs if it still fails

serverError.title: Connection Failed
serverError.checkNetwork: Please check your network or backend service status

diagnose.serviceUnavailable: Reason: Backend service is not running or unreachable
diagnose.devCheck: Debug: Start the backend with: yarn workspace @tx5dr/server dev
diagnose.devLog: Check: Backend logs in the terminal to confirm port 4000 is listening
diagnose.electronCheck: Debug: Restart the app; if still failing, check Electron main process logs
diagnose.prodCheck: Debug: Ensure the backend service is running and listening on /api
diagnose.dockerCheck: Docker: Use docker-compose logs -f to check container logs

appError.title: The interface needs to recover
appError.description: The UI state became inconsistent during the recent mode switch or connection recovery. Try remounting the interface first; if that does not help, refresh the page.

slotPacks.connectFirst: Please connect to TX5DR server first

myFrames.hint: Messages related to me will appear here

spectrum.noData: No spectrum data. Waiting for the stream to recover...
spectrum.digitalWindowEnable: Switch to a fixed view and lock to the most suitable 5 kHz digital window
spectrum.digitalWindowDisable: Currently fixed to the 5 kHz digital window. Click to return to follow view
spectrum.openwebrxDetailEnable: Switch to the OpenWebRX digital detail spectrum view
spectrum.openwebrxDetailDisable: Currently in the OpenWebRX digital detail spectrum view. Click to return to the full spectrum
spectrum.cycleMarkersDescription: Show a subtle line at each digital cycle start

system.title: System Settings
system.ntpDesc: Try NTP servers in list order. The first entry has the highest priority, and the first successful response is shown in the current clock status.
system.ntpPriorityDesc: Drag the handle to reorder. After saving, both background sync and manual measurements will use this exact order.
system.ntpPortHint: Port is fixed to UDP 123. This version supports hostnames or IPv4 addresses only.
system.ntpServerInvalid: Enter a valid hostname or IPv4 address. URLs, paths, and ports are not supported.
system.ntpDefaultTemplateDesc: Restore Official Defaults replaces the current draft with the built-in template below. Click Save to make it effective.
system.allowPublicViewingOnDesc: Unauthenticated users can view as observers, but cannot operate
system.allowPublicViewingOffDesc: Unauthenticated users must log in with a token to access
system.realtimeSettingsCardDesc: Configure realtime audio transport, rtc-data-audio UDP access, and the ws-compat fallback from one place.
system.realtimeSettingsSimpleGuide: Recommended flow: use WebRTC Data Audio by default; LAN/local use usually needs no extra config; for FRP public access, set the external UDP address below and map that UDP port; if UDP cannot connect, clients automatically fall back to ws-compat.
system.realtimeBrowserEntryDesc: By default, clients reuse the current site for /api/realtime/rtc-data-audio signaling. Low-latency audio is selected by ICE over UDP DataChannel and falls back to ws-compat if it fails.
system.realtimeBrowserEntryCurrentLabel: Realtime voice entrypoint currently exposed to clients
system.realtimeFrpHintDesc: For FRP public access, the web entrypoint still uses HTTP/TCP; rtc-data-audio also needs one mapped UDP port. Enter the VPS/public host and UDP port below. If UDP cannot connect, clients automatically fall back to ws-compat.
system.realtimeTransportPolicyDesc: Choose which audio path should be preferred first. Auto tries rtc-data-audio first and falls back to ws-compat when ICE/DataChannel fails. Choose Compatibility Mode if you only want the single-port TCP path.
system.realtimeTransportPolicyCompat: Compatibility Mode (Single Port / Low Local Latency)
system.realtimeTransportPolicyAutoHint: Recommended default. Prefer WebRTC Data Audio first; if UDP/ICE/DataChannel cannot connect, automatically fall back to same-origin ws-compat instead of failing the session.
system.realtimeTransportPolicyCompatHint: Best when only TCP is allowed, UDP mapping is unavailable, enterprise networks block UDP, or you intentionally want a fixed single-port path. This skips rtc-data-audio and always uses ws-compat.
system.realtimeAdminGuideDesc: Shows the active realtime path, UDP endpoint, signaling path, and fallback information for deployment troubleshooting.
system.realtimeCurrentPathHint: This is the server's recommended path based on current configuration and runtime capability. If a client cannot establish rtc-data-audio, it automatically falls back to ws-compat.
system.realtimeNoRecentIssue: No recent realtime audio service issue is currently recorded.
system.realtimePortRequirementsDesc: rtc-data-audio signaling reuses the current site entrypoint. The DataChannel uses one UDP port by default, while ws-compat only needs the current site WebSocket entrypoint.
system.realtimeEffectiveUrlLabel: rtc-data-audio signaling URL exposed to clients
system.realtimePortHintAuto: The low-latency rtc-data-audio path usually needs the current site signaling plus one UDP port. For FRP, set the external UDP address above.
system.realtimePortHintCompat: ws-compat needs no extra UDP port. It reuses the current site WebSocket entrypoint and remains the TCP fallback.
system.realtimePortHintFallback: If UDP mapping is unavailable or wrong, clients try rtc-data-audio and then automatically fall back to ws-compat. Saved external UDP settings affect only new or reconnected sessions.
system.desktopHttpsDesc: Adds a browser/LAN entrypoint for the Electron desktop app. The desktop window itself does not depend on this, but browser access with realtime voice usually needs HTTPS.
system.desktopHttpsPurpose: When do you need this: if you want a phone, tablet, or another computer to open this desktop app in a browser and use realtime voice or microphone features, enabling HTTPS is recommended.
system.desktopHttpsScenarioDesktopTitle: Desktop app only on this machine
system.desktopHttpsScenarioDesktopDesc: You usually do not need HTTPS here. The desktop window can keep working as-is.
system.desktopHttpsScenarioLanDesc: HTTPS is recommended. You can generate a self-signed certificate in one click and manually continue past the browser warning on first visit.
system.desktopHttpsScenarioPublicDesc: Prepare a domain and a properly signed certificate, then import the PEM certificate and private key to avoid browser security warnings.
system.desktopHttpsEnable: Enable HTTPS for browser / LAN access
system.desktopHttpsEnableDesc: Turn this on only when other devices should open this desktop app in a browser and need realtime voice or microphone access.
system.desktopHttpsModeSelfSignedDesc: Best for personal, LAN, or temporary access. Browsers will warn that the certificate is untrusted and you must continue manually.
system.desktopHttpsModeImportedDesc: Best for public, domain-based, or multi-user access. Prepare a PEM certificate file and its matching PEM private key.
system.desktopHttpsRedirect: Automatically redirect external HTTP access to HTTPS
system.desktopHttpsImportRequired: After selecting a proper certificate source, import and validate the PEM certificate and private key before saving.
system.desktopHttpsImportSplitHint: Choose the certificate file (.pem/.crt/.cer) and its matching private key file (.pem/.key) separately. The pair is imported and validated automatically after both files are selected.
system.desktopHttpsImportPartial: One file is still missing. Choose the remaining certificate (.pem/.crt/.cer) or private key (.pem/.key) to validate the pair automatically.
system.desktopHttpsImportVerified: The PEM certificate and private key have been imported and validated.
system.desktopHttpsSelfSignedAuto: No manual upload is needed for self-signed mode. Electron automatically generates or refreshes the local self-signed certificate before starting the HTTPS entrypoint.
system.desktopHttpsStatusDesc: Shows the actual browser entry URL, current certificate status, and the LAN addresses you can share right now.
system.desktopHttpsNoCertificate: No usable certificate yet. Generate a self-signed certificate first, or import a proper certificate.
system.desktopHttpsAccessUrlPending: Shown after you save and enable it
system.desktopHttpsLanHint: Once enabled, both local and LAN access URLs will be shown here.
system.desktopHttpsSelfSignedLimitations: Self-signed limitations: browsers will show an "unsafe" or "untrusted certificate" warning; every device must manually continue on first visit; this is better for personal, internal, or testing use, not for long-term public access.
system.desktopHttpsSelectKeyTitle: Select the PEM private key file
system.decodeWhileTransmittingOffDesc: Stop decoding when any operator is transmitting, avoids residual signals
system.decodeWhileTransmittingOnDesc: Continue decoding during TX cycle, supports dual-cycle remote TX/RX
system.maxSameTransmissionCountDesc: If an operator keeps sending the same FT8/FT4 message, TX-5DR automatically stops that operator so a stuck auto-calling flow does not keep occupying the frequency.
system.spectrumWhileTransmittingOnDesc: Continue displaying spectrum while transmitting, monitor TX signal in real time
system.spectrumWhileTransmittingOffDesc: Pause spectrum analysis while transmitting, reduces CPU usage
system.receiverInfoDesc: If left empty, the first operator's callsign and grid will be used automatically
system.pskrDesc: Automatically report decoded FT8/FT4 signals to
system.decodeWindowDesc: Control the number and timing of decode passes per slot. More windows improve weak signal reception but increase CPU load.
system.closeBehaviorDesc: Default behavior when closing the main window (desktop app only)
system.desktopUpdateDesc: Checks whether a newer build exists. Supported desktop installs download in the background, then show installer progress after restart when needed.
system.desktopUpdateRecentCommitsDesc: The latest update stays visible by default. Expand to inspect the latest five raw commits.

cpuProfile.title: Performance Diagnostics
cpuProfile.desc: If TX5DR feels sluggish, responds slowly, or keeps using a lot of CPU, run a diagnosis and send the result to the developers.
cpuProfile.serverOnlyDesc: Use this when sluggishness or high CPU usage can be reproduced. Start the diagnosis, repeat the action that causes the problem, then finish it as prompted.
cpuProfile.idleTitle: Ready to record one slow moment
cpuProfile.idleDesc: Start here, restart TX5DR when prompted, then reproduce the sluggishness or high CPU usage.
cpuProfile.armedDesc: Restart TX5DR normally. After it starts again, TX5DR will record the problem while you reproduce it.
cpuProfile.runningDesc: Reproduce the sluggishness, slow response, or high CPU usage now. When done, use the recommended action below to finish the diagnosis.
cpuProfile.failedTitle: This diagnosis did not produce a usable result
cpuProfile.failedDesc: This usually happens when TX5DR did not close normally. Start again and use the buttons on this page to restart and finish.
cpuProfile.overrideTitle: Diagnosis is enabled by external configuration
cpuProfile.overrideDesc: This environment has diagnosis enabled outside the app, so the guided flow on this page is read-only.
cpuProfile.completedDesc: Download or copy this file, then include what you were doing, how long the slowdown lasted, and roughly how high CPU usage was when reporting the issue.
cpuProfile.feedback2: 2. The app version and whether you use Desktop, Docker, or a server deployment.
cpuProfile.feedback3: 3. What you were doing when it slowed down and how long it lasted.

start.dev: Restart the server normally to start capture
start.generic: Restart the server normally to start capture

finish.dev: Restart the server normally to finish capture
finish.generic: Restart the server normally to finish capture

system.rtcDataAudioPublicUdpTitle: WebRTC Data Audio external UDP address
system.rtcDataAudioPublicUdpDesc: For FRP/VPS/public mappings. Enter the UDP endpoint browsers can reach from outside, not the LAN IP. Leave host empty to stop publishing public ICE candidates.
system.rtcDataAudioPublicEndpointActive: Currently appending public candidate {{host}}:{{port}}. It takes effect for new or reconnected sessions after saving.
system.rtcDataAudioPublicUdpHint: Empty host disables public candidates; empty port defaults to the local UDP port. Saving does not rebuild active sessions.
system.realtimeRtcDataAudioBenefitsTitle: Default realtime path: WebRTC Data Audio uses a lightweight UDP-like DataChannel for low latency.
system.realtimeRtcDataAudioBenefitsDesc: If DataChannel or UDP cannot connect, TX-5DR falls back to WS/PCM without creating another browser audio runtime.
system.realtimeBrowserEntryRtcHint: Signaling uses the current site WebSocket path. Media frames use rtc-data-audio over WebRTC DataChannel and can fall back to ws-compat.
system.realtimeRtcDataAudioApplyHint: Saved UDP endpoint settings apply to new realtime sessions or reconnects; active listening/capture sessions are not rebuilt automatically.
system.realtimePortHintRtcDataAudio: Expose the fixed UDP port shown here when clients connect from outside the LAN. For FRP, map UDP from the VPS to the same server UDP port.
system.updateWebsiteOnlyDesc: Checks whether a newer build exists. This deployment is updated outside the web UI; use the official website for instructions.
system.desktopUpdateInstallFailed: Failed to prepare the update installer.
system.desktopUpdateManualFallback: This installation does not support automatic updates. Please download the latest version from the official website.

desktopUpdateAutoReason.auto_update_asset_unavailable: This release does not provide an automatic update package for this platform.
desktopUpdateAutoReason.auto_update_requires_nsis_installer: This Windows install was not created by the new NSIS installer. Install the NSIS package manually once to enable future automatic updates.
desktopUpdateAutoReason.auto_update_requires_appimage: Automatic updates on Linux are supported only when running the AppImage package.
desktopUpdateAutoReason.auto_update_requires_signed_zip_install: This macOS install cannot use automatic updates. Install the signed desktop package manually once.
desktopUpdateAutoReason.auto_update_download_url_unavailable: The automatic update package does not have a usable download URL.
desktopUpdateAutoReason.auto_update_metadata_incomplete: The automatic update metadata is incomplete. Use the manual download instead.
desktopUpdateAutoReason.auto_update_semver_not_newer: The updater did not accept this package as a newer version. Use the manual download if needed.
desktopUpdateAutoReason.update_install_not_completed: The previous update did not appear to finish. You can retry or use the manual download.

freqPresets.title: Frequency Preset Management
freqPresets.description: Customize the preset frequencies shown in the frequency selector. Add frequently used frequencies or remove bands you don't use.
freqPresets.resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to restore the default standard frequency presets? Your custom presets will be overwritten.
freqPresets.empty: Preset list is empty. Please add at least one frequency preset.
freqPresets.duplicate: A preset with this frequency already exists
freqPresets.unknownBand: This frequency does not match a known amateur band and will be saved as a custom preset.
freqPresets.repeaterOffsetDisabled: No offset needed when DUP is off
freqPresets.invalidRepeaterOffset: Please enter a valid TX offset
freqPresets.invalidCtcssTone: Please select a valid CTCSS tone
freqPresets.invalidDcsCode: Please select a valid DCS code

clock.title: Clock Calibration
clock.alert: Large offset detected but not applied
clock.unavailable: Server is disconnected, latest clock status is unavailable

remoteAccess.title: Network Access
remoteAccess.description: Access from other devices via browser
remoteAccess.sameNetworkHint: Ensure devices are on the same LAN

config-auth.label: Base config and login
config-auth.description: Read config files and prepare local access tokens

engine-bootstrap.label: Radio engine
engine-bootstrap.description: Assemble the digital radio engine and runtime services

logbook-prewarm.label: Logbook
logbook-prewarm.description: Prepare operator logbooks in the background

active-profile-autostart.label: Radio auto-start
active-profile-autostart.description: Start the current profile from the last configuration

audio-device-discovery.failed: Audio device discovery failed; retry later

logbook-prewarm.timed_out: Logbook preparation is taking longer and continues in the background

ntp-initial-check.failed: Clock calibration service failed to start; retry later

bootstrapStatus.title: Background service startup
bootstrapStatus.subtitle: Ready features remain available. This notice disappears after all services finish.
